era_context: Early Dynastic Period III in Mesopotamia; Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty IV). Akkadian city-states consolidating. Indus Valley Civilization mature phase. Bronze Age expansion.

events.title: Old Kingdom Egypt Dynasty IV pharaonic rule
events.description: Dynasty IV maintained centralized control over Egypt. The state's administrative apparatus, supported by the priesthood and bureaucracy, managed the Nile valley's resources. Monumental construction projects continued, reflecting the state's power and the pharaoh's divine status.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Old Kingdom sites

events.certainty_note: Chronological uncertainty ±20–30 years for Old Kingdom dating.

events.title: Indus Valley Civilization in mature phase
events.description: The Indus Valley Civilization continued in its mature urban phase. Cities were fully developed with sophisticated infrastructure. Trade networks connected the civilization to Mesopotamia and the Persian Gulf. The undeciphered script was in use, and standardized weights and measures facilitated commerce.
events.certainty_note: Radiocarbon dating places mature Indus phase c. 2600–1900 BCE.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence for 2638 BCE. The year is archaeologically indistinguishable from surrounding years.
historiographic_note: This year is part of a stable period in documented civilizations. The historical record is sparse and relies on archaeological evidence. No contemporary written records document 2638 BCE specifically.

graph_edges.note: Indus civilization in stable mature phase
